file_summary: This script installs the 'lup' binary on Linux and macOS by building from source or downloading a pre-built version, and sets up default configuration.

symbols.name: lines_1_40
symbols.summary: This section is the script header with usage instructions and a description of what the script does.

symbols.name: REPO
symbols.summary: The GitHub repository for the lup project; used to clone source code or download pre-built binaries.

symbols.name: BINARY
symbols.summary: The name of the binary being installed; used throughout the script to refer to the executable.

symbols.name: INSTALL_DIR
symbols.summary: The directory where the binary will be installed; defaults to $HOME/.local/bin or can be set via command line argument.

symbols.name: CONFIG_DIR
symbols.summary: The directory where the configuration file will be installed; used in install_config function.

symbols.name: TMP_DIR
symbols.summary: A temporary directory for downloading and building the binary; cleaned up at script exit.

symbols.name: check_existing
symbols.summary: Checks if lup is already installed and warns the user if it is.

symbols.name: build_from_source
symbols.summary: Builds the lup binary from source code, preferring local repository if available or cloning it otherwise.

symbols.name: download_binary
symbols.summary: Downloads a pre-built binary from GitHub Releases if building from source is not possible.

symbols.name: install_config
symbols.summary: Installs a default configuration file if one does not already exist.

symbols.name: main
symbols.summary: The main function that orchestrates the installation process, including platform detection and binary installation.
